title: OHA OMBUDS PROGRAM - 211info
text: OREGON STATE DIVISION OF MEDICAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS OHA OMBUDS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Advocates for individuals by investigating and resolving complaints related to the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) and the public option plan. Provides support with enrollment issues and helps Oregonians access health care providers. Can also assist small businesses 500 Summer Street NE E-49 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (877) 642-0450 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (877) 642-0450 Message line only Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website for information. Call and leave a message or send an email to report a concern. When reporting a concern, callers should include their name, OHP number, email address or phone number, and details about the concern or complaint. Response time is within five business days. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: Government Complaints/Government Ombudsman Offices * Medicaid Recipients Other Services Offered At This Location: FOOD - HEALTH RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS NUTRITION SUPPORTS HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OHP IN-PERSON APPLICATION ASSISTANCE HOUSING/SHELTER - FLEXIBLE SERVICES HOUSING/SHELTER - HEALTH RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS HOUSING SUPPORTS HOME MODIFICATION HOUSING/SHELTER - HEALTH RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS HOUSING SUPPORTS RENTAL ASSISTANCE INFORMATION SERVICES - OREGON HEALTH PLAN CARE COORDINATION NURSE ADVICE LINE MATERIAL GOODS - HEALTH RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS CLIMATE SUPPORTS PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - OHP CLIENT SERVICES UNIT PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - OREGON HEALTH PLAN SPECIALTY MEDICINE - OREGON VETERAN DENTAL PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON STATE DIVISION OF MEDICAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS
